source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Hoohina [i.e. Hoonah], S.E. Alaska, 1884
description: 1884 cyanotype-toned photograph of the Hoonah waterfront village in Southeast Alaska. A row of wooden clan houses and dwellings lines the beach along Port Frederick, with early mission/school buildings visible in the distance. Hoonah (Xu.naa, 'where the north wind doesn't blow') is the Huna Tlingit village that relocated from Glacier Bay during the Little Ice Age.
alt: 1884 sepia-blue photograph of the Hoonah, Alaska waterfront with wooden clan houses along a gravel beach
